controls.title: No macro controls implemented (ML0 baseline)
controls.description: ML0 represents an organisation that has not deployed any macro security controls via Intune. AutoAudit infers ML0 from the absence of ML1 policies — there is no dedicated API call for this state.
controls.notes: ML0 is inferred from the absence of ML1 policies. No collector call is required. A Rego policy can detect ML0 by checking that none of the ML1 controls pass.

controls.title: Macros disabled for standard users
controls.description: Microsoft Office macro execution is disabled for standard users. VBA Macro Notification Settings must be configured to disable macros (with or without notification) for all standard user accounts.
controls.notes: Partial automation — verifies the Intune policy exists and is assigned. Cannot confirm per-device application. ASD confidence: Fair. Setting ID: user_vendor_msft_policy_config_office16v2~policy~l_microsoftofficesystem~l_security~l_vbawarnings (per Office app — Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Visio, Access).

controls.title: Internet-originated macros blocked (Mark of the Web)
controls.description: Macros in files downloaded from the internet are blocked. The Intune policy must configure the block macros from running in Office files from the internet setting.
controls.notes: Partial automation — verifies the Intune policy exists and is assigned. Setting ID unverified — confirm via Microsoft Graph Explorer or ACSC Intune Hardening Guidelines repo before writing Rego. ASD confidence: Fair.

controls.title: AMSI scanning enabled for macros
controls.description: Antimalware Scan Interface (AMSI) scanning is enabled for Office macros, allowing Windows Defender to scan macro code at runtime before execution.
controls.notes: Partial automation — verifies the Intune policy exists and is assigned. Setting ID unverified — confirm via Microsoft Graph Explorer before writing Rego. ASD confidence: Fair.

controls.title: Users cannot change macro settings
controls.description: The macro security policy is enforced such that standard users cannot override or modify the configured macro settings. Verified by confirming the Intune policy is assigned broadly with no exclusions that would allow standard users to change settings.
controls.notes: Partial automation — checks that the policy has a broad assignment (all users or all devices). Cannot verify that no local override mechanism exists on individual devices. ASD confidence: Fair.

controls.title: Win32 API calls blocked from Office macros (ASR rule)
controls.description: The Attack Surface Reduction rule blocking Win32 API calls from Office macros is enabled in Block mode. ASR rule GUID: 92E97FA1-2EDF-4476-BDD6-9DD0B4DDDC7B. Must be Block — Audit mode is non-compliant.
controls.notes: Fully automatable — the ASR rule state (block/audit/off) is directly readable from the Legacy Device Configuration endpoint (/v1.0/deviceManagement/deviceConfigurations). ASD confidence: Good.

controls.title: Only signed macros from trusted publishers can execute
controls.description: Office is configured to allow only digitally signed macros from trusted publishers to execute. Unsigned macros and macros from untrusted publishers are blocked.
controls.notes: Partial automation — verifies the Intune policy exists and is assigned. Cannot confirm that publisher certificates are valid or that the trusted publisher list is current. ASD confidence: Fair.

controls.title: Write access to Trusted Locations restricted to privileged users
controls.description: Only privileged users (administrators) have write access to directories designated as Trusted Locations in Office. Standard users cannot add or modify Trusted Locations.
controls.notes: Not automatable — requires checking file system permissions on Trusted Location directories, which are not exposed via the Microsoft Graph API. Must be verified via manual evidence (e.g. screenshot of folder permissions or Group Policy configuration). Route through Evidence Assistant.

controls.title: Untrusted publishers blocked via Message Bar or Backstage view
controls.description: Office is configured to block macros from untrusted publishers and present the user with the Message Bar or Backstage view rather than silently executing the macro.
controls.notes: Partial automation — verifies the Intune policy exists and is assigned. Setting ID unverified — confirm via Microsoft Graph Explorer before writing Rego. ASD confidence: Fair.

controls.title: V3 macro signatures required
controls.description: Office is configured to require Version 3 (V3) digital signatures for trusted macros, as mandated by the November 2023 ASD Essential Eight revision. V1/V2 signatures are not accepted.
controls.notes: Partial automation — verifies the Intune policy is set to require V3 signatures. Cannot verify that existing signing certificates are V3-compliant. Setting ID unverified — confirm via Graph Explorer before writing Rego. ASD confidence: Fair.

controls.title: Pre-signing malicious code check performed
controls.description: A process is in place to check macros for malicious code before they are signed and added to the trusted publisher list. This is a governance process control, not a technical setting.
controls.notes: Not automatable — governance process control with no API equivalent. Must be demonstrated through written attestation or process documentation. Route through Evidence Assistant.

controls.title: Annual trusted publisher list review
controls.description: The organisation performs an annual review of all trusted macro publishers to confirm they are still valid, necessary, and have not been compromised.
controls.notes: Not automatable — requires evidence of a completed annual review (e.g. a dated review document or signed attestation). Route through Evidence Assistant.
